summary: Implemented Sheik's SpecialAirHi animation, physics, and collision callbacks in src/melee/ft/chara/ftSeak/ftSk_SpecialHi.c, replacing placeholder decomp stubs. The PR also refined ftSeakAttributes field types in types.h so the new functions could fully match: x4C, x58, and x5C were changed from s32 to f32 with comments documenting the match dependency. Review pushed the patch toward proper header inclusion instead of local extern declarations and discouraged leaving decomp.me scratch-link comments in source unless they add missing technical information.
what_changed: Added required includes in ftSk_SpecialHi.c for functions used by the new decompiles: ft/ft_0892.h, ft/ft_0D14.h, and ftCommon/ftCo_FallSpecial.h.
Removed the local static forward declaration for ft_8008A2BC after review feedback indicated the function should come from its owning header.
Implemented ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Anim: reads ftSeakAttributes from fp->dat_attrs, waits until ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining returns 0, then calls ftCo_80096900 with attribute values x58 and x5C.

Implemented ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Phys: if fp->cmd_vars[0] is set, applies air drift helpers ftCommon_8007D4B8 and ftCommon_8007D440 using attributes->x4C times fp->co_attrs.air_drift_max; otherwise damps vertical velocity using ftSk_Init_804D9694, then calls ftCommon_8007CEF4.

Implemented ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Coll: chooses ledge_grab_dir from fp->facing_dir compared with ftSk_Init_804D9660, checks ground/ledge with ft_CheckGroundAndLedge, transitions through ftCo_800D5CB0 with attributes->x5C on success, and otherwise calls ftCliffCommon_80081298.
Updated ftSeakAttributes in types.h: x4C, x58, and x5C changed from s32 to f32, with comments tying those type choices to full matches for ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Phys and ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Anim.
smart_moves: Used analogous Zelda/Sheik special-hi behavior as a comparison source, according to the PR body, which is a common and useful fighter decomp strategy when move logic is mirrored across characters.
Validated struct field typing through matching pressure: changing ftSeakAttributes x4C, x58, and x5C to f32 directly explained the generated code needed by the new SpecialAirHi functions.

Reused existing ftCommon and collision helpers instead of open-coding movement or landing behavior, preserving engine conventions around air drift, fall special transitions, ground/ledge checks, and cliff interaction.
Responded to review by moving away from ad-hoc function declarations toward canonical owning headers.
decomp_lessons: When a fighter attribute field participates in floating-point arithmetic or is passed to APIs expecting floats, a struct field typed as s32 can block full matching; verify dat_attrs field types against use sites and generated code.

For new decompiled functions, include the headers that declare called helpers rather than adding local extern declarations in the .c file. Review specifically cited ft_8008A2BC as belonging to ft/ft_0892.h.

Scratch links may be useful during development, but reviewer guidance says they are not an encouraged source-code pattern. If the scratch contains missing technical information, preserve that information as a meaningful comment instead of linking the scratch.

Comparing to similar character code, here Zelda-related SpecialHi behavior, can guide implementation of Sheik special-move callbacks, but the final code should still be grounded in matching and local attribute layout.

Small local padding temporaries such as u8 _[8] appeared in the implemented Phys and Coll functions, indicating stack-shape matching tactics were used; future work should confirm whether such padding is still required after surrounding type/header cleanup.
naming_conventions: Sheik files use the ftSk_ prefix, e.g. ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Anim, ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Phys, ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Coll, while common fighter functions use ftCommon_ or ftCo_ prefixes.
Unidentified Sheik attribute fields remain named by offsets such as x4C, x58, and x5C, but comments can document inferred type and matching role.
The fighter attribute struct is named ftSeakAttributes despite the Sheik gameplay name, matching the existing ftSeak directory and codebase convention.
Review examples reinforce header ownership naming: ft_8008A2BC should be declared via ft/ft_0892.h, not locally in ftSk_SpecialHi.c.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed ftSeakAttributes.x4C from s32 to f32 to make ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Phys fully match when multiplying by fp->co_attrs.air_drift_max.
Changed ftSeakAttributes.x58 and x5C from s32 to f32 to make ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Anim fully match when passing them into ftCo_80096900.
Used explicit temporary floats x and y in ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Anim and vel_y in ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Phys, likely to shape codegen for matching.
Inserted u8 _[8] stack padding in ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Phys and ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Coll, suggesting local stack-layout pressure in the match.

Relied on existing engine calls such as ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining, ftCo_80096900, ftCommon_8007D4B8, ftCommon_8007D440, ftCommon_8007CEF4, ft_CheckGroundAndLedge, ftCo_800D5CB0, and ftCliffCommon_80081298 rather than synthesizing behavior.
review_feedback: ribbanya requested using the proper headers for referenced functions instead of declaring them directly in ftSk_SpecialHi.c; example: ft_8008A2BC comes from ft/ft_0892.h.

ribbanya discouraged adding decomp.me scratch-link comments to source as a general pattern. If a scratch contains missing information, that information should be captured directly in comments; attribution is handled by git authorship.
Gelatart acknowledged the scratch-link guidance and said they would refrain going forward.
Gelatart also responded to the header-declaration feedback with 'Fixing that and pushing.'
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove or revise decomp.me scratch-link comments in ftSk_SpecialHi.c.
Check whether ftSeakAttributes fields x4C, x58, and x5C receive semantic names in subsequent decomp work.
Search for other fighter SpecialAirHi implementations, especially Zelda, to compare attribute layout and callback structure.
Audit ftSk_SpecialHi.c for remaining local declarations that should be replaced by owning headers.
Verify whether the u8 _[8] padding locals in ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Phys and ftSk_SpecialAirHi_Coll remain necessary after later matching/type cleanup.
